protocol.purpose: Replace the paper's previously-admitted biased qualitative check ('3 of 17 full-depth flagged cases, selected for clarity of the judge's note') with a seeded, non-cherry-picked random audit of Gemini-3.1-Pro's visible_defect=yes flags on the band-position LoRA ablation (30-subject x 2-seed dev probe set, step4000), plus a false-negative check on visible_defect=no cases.
framing_and_honesty: This audit was performed by a Claude-family model (Sonnet) reading the actual generated images and independently classifying each sampled Gemini flag. This is model-checking-model across two different model families (Claude checking Gemini), NOT an independent human review. The contact sheet PNGs exist so a human can do the final ~5-minute eyeball check themselves.

The seed (0) and the full sampling code were fixed and written down BEFORE any sampled image was opened/viewed. No image was viewed prior to sampling, no case was excluded or swapped after being viewed, and no replacement sampling occurred.

Per-case verdicts were formed by reading each full-resolution source PNG individually (1024x1024 native), NOT by reading the downscaled contact-sheet cells (~480px) -- the contact sheet is a human-facing summary artifact only, it was not used to form these verdicts.

Verdicts judge visible_defect specifically (anatomical/geometric/composition breakage visible in the generated image itself), not same_character identity mismatch, which is a separate judge field and out of scope here -- a case can be same_character=no/partial (wrong character) while visible_defect=no (cleanly rendered), and vice versa.

full_depth (0-29, r4).source_of_mapping: probe/58_band_ablation_run.sh (run_arm function, ckptlink naming) + verified 1:1 against all 60 judge keys

late (14-29, r8).source_of_mapping: probe/58_band_ablation_run.sh + verified 1:1 against all 60 judge keys

early (0-15, r8) -- NOT sampled, see not.source_of_mapping: probe/58_band_ablation_run.sh + verified 1:1 against all 60 judge keys

mid (10-25, r8).source_of_mapping: probe/59_band_ablation_report.py docstring + probe/60_crossjudge_gpt55.py MID= path string, both confirm base_rep30_step4000 IS the pre-existing mid=10-25,r8 arm (script comment: 'mid 10-25 r8 already exists as run_mix2/step_4000' -- base_rep30_step4000 is that same run under its dev-probe output name). Verified 1:1 against all 60 judge keys.

protocol.judge_error_note: 6 of 240 judge entries across the 4 arms (2 late, 1 early, 3 mid) are API-call failures (HTTP 429 rate limit, or judge response with no parseable JSON) -- these have no same_character/visible_defect verdict at all and were excluded from both the yes/no populations and the sampling universe (they are neither 'yes' nor 'no'). This slightly shrinks effective denominators, e.g. mid is 2 yes / 55 no / 3 error out of 60, not 2 yes / 58 no.
protocol.early_arm_excluded_note: The early (0-15, r8) arm's judge file was inspected for schema and population counts (5 visible_defect=yes out of 60) for completeness, but Step 2 of the assigned protocol does not call for sampling from it (only full-depth, late, and mid are named as sample sources). No early-arm cases appear in the sample or contact sheets; this is a faithful implementation of the given protocol, not an omission.
protocol.sampling_order: 1) sample 10 of 17 full-depth visible_defect=yes (or all if <=10); 2) sample 5 of 11 late visible_defect=yes (or all if <=5); 3) take ALL mid visible_defect=yes (n=2, no randomness consumed); 4) sample 4 of 55 mid visible_defect=no as negative controls. This exact order determines the random.Random(0) draw stream -- reordering would change the sample.
protocol.sampling_code:
import json, random, os

DATA = "data"

def load(fname):
    return json.load(open(os.path.join(DATA, fname)))

full_depth = load("gemini_judge_band_0-29_r4_step4000.json")
late = load("gemini_judge_band_14-29_r8_step4000.json")
mid = load("gemini_judge_devprobe_base_rep30_step4000.json")

# Deterministic universes: sort case-id strings alphabetically so the sample
# does not depend on JSON key insertion order (which reflects concurrent
# judge-call completion order, not anything meaningful).
fd_yes = sorted(k for k, v in full_depth.items() if v.get("visible_defect") == "yes")
late_yes = sorted(k for k, v in late.items() if v.get("visible_defect") == "yes")
mid_yes = sorted(k for k, v in mid.items() if v.get("visible_defect") == "yes")
mid_no = sorted(k for k, v in mid.items() if v.get("visible_defect") == "no")

rng = random.Random(0)

# Fixed draw order: full-depth flags, then late flags, then mid negative
# controls. (mid_yes is taken in full -- only 2 exist -- so it does not
# consume any randomness.)
sample_fd = rng.sample(fd_yes, min(10, len(fd_yes)))
sample_late = rng.sample(late_yes, min(5, len(late_yes)))
sample_mid_yes = list(mid_yes)  # ALL mid-arm flags, no sampling needed (n=2)
sample_mid_no = rng.sample(mid_no, min(4, len(mid_no)))
protocol.image_existence_check: Before sampling, verified all 240 judge entries (60 per arm x 4 arms) resolve to an existing PNG file via the filename pattern above (0 missing). After sampling, re-verified all 21 sampled image paths exist (0 missing) -- no replacement sampling was needed.

cases.judge_note: Intrusive, garbled foreground element on the left side.
cases.reason: Left-foreground has an out-of-focus blob (blonde hair/white fabric) that reads as intrusive/unclear; could be intentional bokeh framing, not anatomical breakage, but genuinely odd on a quick look.

cases.judge_note: Face is garbled due to struggling with anthro design.
cases.reason: Anthro rat/mouse face is a coherent, well-formed deliberate design (symmetric eyes, nose, whiskers, cap) -- not garbled; judge's 'struggling with anthro design' reads as unfamiliarity with the design, not real breakage.

cases.judge_note: Hand grabbing the shirt is severely mangled.
cases.reason: Hand gripping the shirt collar is a bit soft/indistinct in finger separation but is not 'severely mangled' -- plausible finger count and pose, just stylistically soft rendering.

cases.judge_note: Different skin tone, eyes, hair style, and accessories. Impossible torso anatomy.
cases.reason: No impossible torso anatomy visible; waist/hip curve is stylized (exaggerated proportions) but coherent and single, with no extra/missing/twisted body parts. Skin/eye/hair mismatch cited in the same note is a same_character issue, not a defect.

cases.judge_note: Identity features match, but image 2 has severely garbled anatomy and clothing.
cases.reason: One odd dark thread/strand dangles unattached near the shoulder patch and the jacket's cracked-texture folds are visually busy, but the face/hand/gesture are coherent -- not 'severely garbled' as claimed.

cases.judge_note: Character matches well, but arm/hand anatomy is garbled.
cases.reason: Her left arm (image right side) drapes the jacket but terminates with no recognizable hand -- the sleeve dissolves into an ambiguous rolled/cylindrical fold with zero fingers visible where a hand should be. Matches the judge's 'hand is missing' claim.

cases.judge_note: Garbled hand anatomy in image 2; differing hair color and gender presentation.
cases.reason: Hand holding a white cloth/paper at hip level is a blob with only vague finger-like ridges, no clean individual digits -- genuinely melted/garbled hand anatomy.

cases.judge_note: Half of the hair is inexplicably long and blonde.
cases.reason: A long straight blonde hair strand is spliced into otherwise wavy brown hair -- a visually jarring rendering-consistency break (two incompatible hair textures/colors fused) that any viewer would immediately notice as wrong, though it's a compositing artifact rather than body anatomy.

cases.judge_note: Image 2 shows a human wearing a mask, not the anthro rabbit character.
cases.reason: The bunny mask is unsettling but cleanly and coherently rendered (symmetric, consistent lighting/linework, sits properly on neck); body proportions and visible hand near the belt are normal. This is an identity/design-departure (human in a mask replacing the anthro rabbit) captured correctly by same_character=no, not a rendering defect.

cases.judge_note: Different hair color and gender presentation; mangled bottom-right hand.
cases.reason: Hand holding the phone (near face) is acceptable; the lower-right hanging hand is a bit stiff/waxy with unclear finger separation but no clear extra/missing digit -- a soft imperfection, not obviously 'mangled'.

cases.judge_note: Garbled/melted anatomy on the right arm.
cases.reason: The right arm (image right) blurs into an indistinct pale/translucent shape against the white background near the elbow/forearm, with no recognizable hand -- genuinely melted-looking limb.

cases.judge_note: Face horizontally stretched. Headband missing.
cases.reason: Face reads as a normal, if rounder/cuter, stylized anime face -- no horizontal stretching or warping visible in eyes/nose/mouth alignment. Missing headband is a same_character issue, not a defect.

cases.judge_note: Garbled hand anatomy.
cases.reason: Hand near the hoodie drawstring toggle is a little blob-like around the small object, but the overall grip/pose is plausible -- a soft/minor imperfection, not clearly broken.

cases.judge_note: Different skin tone and added scar; hand on shoulder is garbled.
cases.reason: Hand resting on the opposite shoulder shows a normal grip with distinguishable fingers and thumb -- reads as an ordinary, if slightly stylized, hand-on-shoulder pose, not garbled.

cases.judge_note: Missing elf ears; generated image has three arms/hands.
cases.reason: Exactly two arms and two hands are visible (one raised to cheek, one holding a small item at the belt) -- no third arm/hand anywhere in the frame. The 'three arms/hands' claim does not hold up; likely the judge misread the layered strap/pouch costume details as an extra limb.

cases.judge_note: Different hair color and gender presentation; hand anatomy is garbled.
cases.reason: Hand holding the phone near the face is soft-shaded with somewhat indistinct finger boundaries, but the overall hand shape and finger count read as plausible -- a mild imperfection, not clearly garbled.

cases.judge_note: Characters match well, but image 2 has strange object on chest and a weirdly placed hand.
cases.reason: A silver oval charm/pendant hangs oddly on the chest (unusual accessory placement, not an anatomical defect) and the lower-left hand resting on a surface is cropped abruptly at the frame edge with a slightly stiff pose -- genuinely a bit odd, but not severe breakage.

cases.judge_note: Missing signature swirly eyebrow, facial hair, and asymmetrical eye-covering hairstyle.
cases.reason: Face/neck/shoulders all anatomically normal; no hands in frame; nothing resembling damage. Judge's 'no' is correct -- no false negative.

cases.judge_note: Gender presentation differs significantly; earrings and styling also mismatch.
cases.reason: Face, neck, shoulders, collarbone all normal; no hands in frame; nothing resembling damage. Judge's 'no' is correct -- no false negative.

cases.judge_note: Missing red headband in generated image.
cases.reason: Hand adjusting collar/ribbon is a little soft but plausible (visible fingers, thumb); face/eyes normal. Judge's 'no' is correct -- no false negative.

cases.judge_note: Completely different facial features, age, skin tone, and eye color.
cases.reason: Face/neck/torso proportionate; hands at bottom edge are cropped but show plausible fist shapes. Judge's 'no' is correct -- no false negative.

negative_control_false_negative_check.method: Each of the 4 mid-arm visible_defect=no cases was visually inspected in full for anatomical/geometric/composition damage the judge might have missed (a false negative), independent of the identity-mismatch notes the judge recorded for same_character.
negative_control_false_negative_check.result: 0 of 4 negative controls show any obvious damage the judge missed -- all 4 are clean renders (normal anatomy, no melted/missing limbs, no extra body parts). The judge's visible_defect=no calls on this sample are correct.

mid_flags.arm: mid (10-25, r8) -- ALL visible_defect=yes cases (not a sample)

summary.overall_read: PARTIAL SUPPORT, with an important caveat about judge precision. (1) Real damage is not judge noise: full-depth's flagged sample contains 3/10 unambiguous CONFIRMED severe defects (a hand fully dissolving into fabric folds with no fingers, a hand reduced to a featureless blob gripping cloth, and a jarring two-texture hair splice) plus 4/10 BORDERLINE real-but-mild oddities -- 7/10 (70%) of sampled full-depth flags correspond to something genuinely visible in the image, not nothing. The 4 mid-arm negative controls are all clean (0/4 false negatives), so the judge is not just flagging at random regardless of image content. (2) The full-depth arm's CONFIRMED rate (3/10=30%) is the highest of the three arms sampled (late 1/5=20%, mid 0/2=0%), and its confirmed cases include the most severe failure mode observed in this audit (limbs terminating with no hand at all) -- this directionally supports the paper's ranking (full-depth worse than mid), though n=10/5/2 is too small for a precise statistical claim. (3) But the judge is meaningfully noisy: FALSE-POSITIVE rate is 3/10 (30%) in full-depth and 3/5 (60%) in late -- most strikingly, one late-arm flag ('three arms/hands') is simply factually wrong (the image clearly has two), and multiple flags in both arms mislabel coherent-but-unusual character designs (an anthro rat face, a bunny mask) as 'garbled' when they are actually clean, deliberate renders -- an identity/uncanniness-driven false alarm pattern, not anatomy breakage. CONCLUSION: the paper's claim that the full-depth defect gradient reflects real damage (not pure judge artifact) holds up on this audit -- but the raw 17/11/... visible_defect=yes COUNTS overstate true severe-defect rates by roughly a third to two-thirds depending on arm, and the paper should not present those raw counts as if every flag were a severe defect. Applying this sample's CONFIRMED-or-BORDERLINE ('real, not nothing') rate per arm to each arm's full flagged population as a rough precision correction: full-depth ~17*0.70=~12 real-ish cases, late ~11*0.40=~4-5, mid =2 (both of its only 2 flags real-but-mild) -- the gradient direction (full-depth > late > mid) survives the correction, and the 4 clean negative controls further argue against 'it's all noise'. This is a directional read only (n=10/5/2 per arm), not a replacement for a full re-annotation. Recommend the paper cite this audit's CONFIRMED-only and CONFIRMED-or-BORDERLINE rates alongside (or instead of) the raw judge flag counts, and note the two concrete judge false-positive patterns found (uncanny-but-coherent character designs; occasional outright hallucinated content).
